title: Fabrication of hydrophobic, mechanically flexible, and optically transparent polyimide aerogels
abstract: The present disclosure provides a method of fabrication of physically crosslinked polyimide (PCPI) aerogels with significantly improved properties using no chemical crosslinker. The fabricated PCPI aerogels present high moisture resistance, hydrophobic behavior, ultralow density, ultrahigh porosity, excellent thermal stability, increased mechanical strength, and high mechanical flexibility. Also, samples have been successfully fabricated in a thin-film geometry with high mechanical flexibility and controlled thickness. Furthermore, some fabricated aerogel films present improved optical transparency of over 80%, which is the highest transparency reported so far for organic PI aerogels.
claims:
Claims THEREFORE WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A polyimide aerogel, comprising: a polyimide-based polymer of the formula 1 : Formula 1 wherein n is an integer equal to, or higher than 10, and wherein the polymer is physically crosslinked through polymer chain entanglement, and formed by a process including initial gelation for about 1 to about 10 minutes; and wherein the aerogel is not a significant source of polyamic amide polymer, alternatively, the aerogel is free of polyamic amide polymer. 2. The polyimide aerogel according to claim 1 , wherein the aerogel has a pore size in a range from about 2 nanometers to about 100 nanometers. 3. The polyimide aerogel according to claims 1 or 2, wherein the aerogel has pores with a pore size of less than about 2 nanometers. 4. The polyimide aerogel according to claims 1 , 2 or 3, wherein the aerogel has a porosity in a range from about 85% to about 99%. 5. The polyimide aerogel according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the polyimide aerogel is free of chemical crosslinkers. 6. The polyimide aerogel according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the aerogel has a density in a range from about 0.07 g/cm 3 to about 0.25 g/cm 3 . 7. The polyimide aerogel according to any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the aerogel has a thermal conductivity in a range from about 15 to about 50 milliwatts/degrees Kelvin-meters. 8. The polyimide aerogel according to any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the aerogel is characterized by a hydrophobicity in a range from about 90 degrees to about 140 degrees water contact angle. 9. The polyimide aerogel according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the aerogel is characterized by a water uptake as low as about 1 %. 10. The polyimide aerogel according to any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the aerogel has an onset of thermal decomposition in a range from about 500 to about 700Â°C. 11 . The polyimide aerogel according to any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the aerogel has a dielectric constant in a range from about 1 .5 to about 3. 12. The polyimide aerogel according to any one of claims 1 to 11 , wherein the aerogel is in the form of a film having a thickness of at least about 80 microns, and wherein the aerogel has optical transparency in a range from about 60% to about 99%. 13. The polyimide aerogel according to any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the aerogel is characterized by a compressive modulus in a range from about 17 to 25 Mega Pascal (MPa). 14. The polyimide aerogel according to any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the aerogel is characterized by a tensile modulus in a range from about 1 to 5 Mega Pascal (MPa). 15. The polyimide aerogel according to any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the integer n is in a range between about 10 to about 60, from about 10 to about 50, or from about 30 to about 50. 16. A process of making a polyimide aerogel of any one of claims 1 to 15, comprising the steps of â¢ forming a wet gel by reacting a diamine monomer (DIAM) with a dianhydride monomer (DIAH) in a reaction solution comprising a dipolar aprotic solvent to form the polymer of Formula A; wherein n is an integer equal to, or higher than 10 Formula A; â¢ imidizing the formed polymer; â¢ gelling the formed polymer for about 1 to 10 minutes; â¢ replacing the aprotic solvent with a drying solvent; and â¢ removing the drying solvent from the formed wet gel. 17. The process of claim 16, wherein the step of imidizing the formed 

polymer is performed through thermal imidization or chemical imidization. 18. The process of claim 16 or 17, further comprising the step of aging the wet gel obtained by the initial gelation to increase the polymerization yield and to advance the degree of physical crosslinking. 19. The process of claim 16, 17 or 18, wherein the aprotic solvent is N- methylpyrrolidinone (NMP), dimethylformamide (DMF), dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), or a mixture of NMP and tetrahydrofuran (THF). 20. The process of any one of claims 16 to 19, wherein the step of reacting the DIAM monomer with the DIAH monomer is done without the use of a chemical crosslinker. 21 . The process of any one of claims 16 to 20, wherein the DIAM monomer and DIAH monomer are first dissolved separately then the DIAM monomer solution is combined with the DIAH monomer solution. 22. The process of any one of claims 16 to 21 , wherein DIAM monomer is first dissolved and DIAH monomer is added to the DIAM solution. 23. The process of any one of claims 16 to 22, wherein the drying solvent is removed via supercritical drying, freeze drying, or ambient pressure drying. 24. The process of any one of claims 16 to 23, wherein the wet gel forming step occurs over a period of time ranging between about 1 minute to about 24 hours. 25. The process of any one of claims 16 to 24, wherein the integer n is in a range between about 10 to about 60, from 10 to about 50, or from about 30 to about 50. 26. The process of any one of claims 16 to 25, wherein the diamine 

monomer (DIAM) is 2,2â-dimethylbenzidine (DMBZ) or 4,4'-oxydianiline (ODA). 27. The process of claim 26, wherein the diamine monomer (DIAM) is 2,2â- dimethylbenzidine (DMBZ). 28. The process of claim 26, wherein the diamine monomer (DIAM) is 4,4'- oxydianiline (ODA). 29. The process of any one of claims 16 to 28, wherein the dianhydride monomer (DIAH) is biphenyl-tetracarboxylic acid dianhydride (BPDA). 30. The process of any one of claims 16 to 25, wherein the diamine monomer (DIAM) is a combination of both 2,2â-dimethylbenzidine (DMBZ) and 4,4'-oxydianiline (ODA). 31 . The process of any one of claims 16 to 30, wherein the integer n is in a range between from about 10 to about 60, from 10 to about 50, or from about 30 to about 50.
